parsePreview.description: Preview the result of filename parsing in real-time.

instructions.docsLink: For more detailed syntax, please refer to the {link}.
lines: Use curly braces {'{'}{'}'} to define a field to capture.
Add a type specifier after a colon, e.g., {'{'}episode:d{'}'} to capture a number.
The destination rule can use format specifiers, e.g., {'{'}episode:02d{'}'} to pad with zeros.
Click the example buttons above to load examples.
instructions.title: Usage Instructions

parsePreview.title: Parse Preview

instructions.title: Instructions
lines: Use parentheses () in 'Filename Regex' to create capture groups.
Use \1, \2, \3, etc., in 'Rename Regex' to reference corresponding groups.
E.g., (\d{'{'}2{'}'}) captures two digits, referenced with \1.
You can modify the test filename above to validate different cases.
Click the test case buttons above to load examples.

regexPreview.title: Regex Preview
regexPreview.usage3: captures four digits, like a year
